meaning: n.雇用骗子,"托"v.为...当雇用骗子
examples: He is tarnishing his reputation by being a shill for the tobacco industry.
I shill had the long hair that you like.
"My guess is, you'll have a chance to buy these names cheaper," says Shill.
Even lower down on the list, artists are getting paid considerable sums to shill products.
Tellingly, money managers who haven't the time or the need to shill themselves are seldom the performers.
